title: Tectaria kehdingiana
taxonomy: Tectaria kehdingiana is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Tectaria, which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Tectaria kehdingiana is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its lacy fronds and distinctive leaf shape. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large group of ferns that are found in a wide range of habitats. Tectaria kehdingiana is an example of a tropical fern that is adapted to life in the understory of dense forests.
uses: Tectaria kehdingiana has been found to have potential medicinal uses, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
